title: Generative Contradiction — A Computational Exhibit
description:
Computational exhibit of three modes of contradiction (destructive, tolerative, generative) realised by sending the same self-referential diagonal seed through Classical propositional logic, Priest's Logic of Paradox (LP), and Gödel–Löb provability logic (GL). The same diagonal seed launches a strict unbounded hierarchy in one environment and provably nothing in the others.

The artifact bundles a GL prover (signed labelled tableau cross-checked with finite-Kripke search), a fixed-point engine (de Jongh–Sambin / Boolos 1993) with an independent verifier, classical and LP evaluators, a Con_n ladder with machine-verified countermodels (and minimality exhaustively verified for n ≤ 5 as of v0.3.0), and an auto-generated headline table.

Version 0.2.0 adds two optional WP6 control artifacts: a finite Kripke-style fixed-point model for a transparent truth predicate over LP / strong-Kleene (E-C1, first-order upgrade of the LP quarantine experiment), and a Lean 4 instantiation of the strict stage-1 strengthening IΣ_1 ⹲ IΣ_1 + Con(IΣ_1) via FormalizedFormalLogic/Foundation (E-C2).

Version 0.3.0 fills in the Henkin contrast (E-A3): the same fixed-point engine fed ¬□p versus □p produces Con_0 (which launches the Con_n hierarchy when adjoined) versus ⊤ (which adds nothing when adjoined), under one shared GL-equivalence measure on the letterless fragment. It also climbs the arithmetic-side Lean ladder to stage 10 by stitching a one-line bridge per stage out of Foundation's existing Entailment.WeakerThan.trans, all under the same four-axiom set as stage 1 (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, ISigma1_delta1Definable). The GL-side minimality enumeration is bumped to n ≤ 5, and both ladders' walls (Foundation's instance-graph shape vs. exponential GL frame enumeration) are recorded in experiments/wp6/CLIMB_REPORT.md.

Illustration, not proof, of the philosophical thesis.
